reasoning: The 1500m freestyle long course world record is an extremely difficult record that typically stands for about a decade (Sun Yang held it for 12 years; Grant Hackett for 10 years). Bobby Finke set the current record in August 2024. Long-course records are almost overwhelmingly broken at major summer peaking meets (Olympics or World Championships). With no Long Course World Championships or Olympic Games scheduled between November 2025 and February 2026, the competitive environment makes a record-breaking swim highly improbable. Furthermore, there is no evidence of doping investigations that could retroactively strip him of the record. Following the prompt's instruction to keep probabilities between 0.05 and 0.95, making 0.95 the upper bound, I am capping the near-term forecast at 0.95 and slightly lowering it as the time horizon increases.
